title: eb-agent catalog ref-set walkers — env.servers[] + externalServices[] across coverage_infrastructure + dangling_resource_refs
description: Extends catalog ref-set walker pattern to walk env.servers[].id and catalog.externalServices[].id across BOTH tools that build a global catalog_refs set: coverage_infrastructure.py (v1.0.0 OUT-1, OUT-2) and dangling_resource_refs.py (v1.1.0 OUT-3, OUT-4). v1.0.0 spec scope was limited to coverage_infrastructure.py based on the symptom that surfaced first (verify check 3). v1.1.0 amendment expands scope after re-verify surfaced same root cause in dangling_resource_refs.py walker. Substrate read of all 9 eb-agent tools confirms only these 2 build a global catalog ref-set; the 7 trace + dangling-credential tools do not share this pattern.

outputs.description: Extend catalog_refs builder in coverage_infrastructure.py to walk env.get('servers', []) collecting srv.get('id') values. Insertion point: between the existing env.objectStorage[] walk and the services[] walk, inside the for-env loop. Walk shape mirrors existing objectStorage walk. Closes the FK resolution gap for accessGrants and hostPosture entries referencing server ids that exist nested under environments per infrastructure-catalog.schema.json.
acceptanceCriteria: coverage_infrastructure.py contains a walk over env.get('servers', []) that adds srv.get('id') to catalog_refs inside the environments loop
Walk only adds non-falsy ids (guard with if srv.get('id'): pattern matching existing walks)
No other behavior changes - same ToolResult shape, same summary string format

outputs.description: Extend catalog_refs builder in coverage_infrastructure.py to walk catalog.get('externalServices', []) collecting svc.get('id') values. Insertion point: after the existing databases[] walk, parallel to services[] walk. accessGrant.resourceType enum includes 'external-service' so externalServices FK targets are canonical resourceRefs.
acceptanceCriteria: coverage_infrastructure.py contains a walk over catalog.get('externalServices', []) that adds svc.get('id') to catalog_refs

outputs.description: Extend catalog_refs builder in dangling_resource_refs.py to walk env.get('servers', []) collecting srv.get('id') values. Same insertion point and shape as OUT-1 (between objectStorage walk and services walk inside for-env loop). Closes the FK resolution gap for accessGrants referencing nested server ids — currently dangling_resource_refs.py reports server-cis-services-1 and server-do-primary as dangling despite both being present in env.servers[].
acceptanceCriteria: dangling_resource_refs.py contains a walk over env.get('servers', []) that adds srv.get('id') to catalog_refs inside the environments loop
Walk only adds non-falsy ids (guard with if srv.get('id'): pattern matching existing walks)
No other behavior changes — same ToolResult shape, same summary string format
eb-agent dangling-resourceRefs returns 0 dangling against d-2026-06-03-eb-infra-hygiene v1.1.0 substrate

outputs.description: Extend catalog_refs builder in dangling_resource_refs.py to walk catalog.get('externalServices', []) collecting svc.get('id') values. Same insertion point and shape as OUT-2 (after databases walk). Symmetric with OUT-2 in coverage_infrastructure.py.
acceptanceCriteria: dangling_resource_refs.py contains a walk over catalog.get('externalServices', []) that adds svc.get('id') to catalog_refs

preMerge: python3 -c 'from agent.tools.coverage_infrastructure import CoverageInfrastructure; from agent.tools.dangling_resource_refs import DanglingResourceRefs; print(CoverageInfrastructure.name, DanglingResourceRefs.name)' — exit 0 (both modules load, both classes present)
eb-agent coverage-infrastructure returns ok=true with grantNotInCatalog=[] and postureNotInCatalog=[] against d-2026-06-03-eb-infra-hygiene v1.1.0 substrate
eb-agent dangling-resourceRefs returns ok=true with 0 dangling against d-2026-06-03-eb-infra-hygiene v1.1.0 substrate
eb-agent dangling-credentialRefs returns ok=true with 0 dangling (unchanged — that tool does not touch the catalog walker)
git diff --stat scoped to exactly 2 files (coverage_infrastructure.py, dangling_resource_refs.py)
PR CI green; --admin merge forbidden per memory #15
postMerge: eb-agent coverage-infrastructure on main reflects the walker extension
eb-agent dangling-resourceRefs on main reflects the walker extension
Unblocks d-2026-06-03-eb-infra-hygiene v1.1.0 verify.sh checks 3 AND 4

amended to v1.1.0 after infra-hygiene verify check 4 surfaced same root cause in dangling_resource_refs.py — substrate read of all 9 eb-agent tools confirmed 2-file walker-fix scope; amendment expands scopeFiles + adds OUT-3/OUT-4 covering dangling_resource_refs.py

notes: Source files read 2026-06-03: dangling_resource_refs.py, dangling_credential_refs.py, trace_workflow.py, trace_service.py, trace_resource.py, trace_environment.py, trace_bucket.py. Walker pattern audit: coverage_infrastructure.py and dangling_resource_refs.py are the ONLY tools that build a global catalog_refs set walking all environments + top-level collections. The 5 trace tools and dangling_credential_refs.py either don't read infrastructure-catalog (trace_workflow, trace_resource, dangling_credential_refs) or read it for targeted lookups not requiring a global ref-set walk (trace_bucket: specific bucket lookup; trace_environment: single env fetch by id; trace_service: string-match across services[]). v1.1.0 scope is exhaustive for the walker class — no remaining tools share the gap. Per F0B85LNSTC4 doctrine + memory #20 enterprise-complete: amendment scope captures every tool with the gap in one PR, not iteratively across multiple PRs. Spec instance v1.0.0 currently anchored at SR; v1.1.0 amendment requires SR re-land via succession micro-PR. Doctrine note: this is the fifth amendment loop today (Cat 3 partial paths, infra-hygiene v1.1.0 buckets-vs-grants, infra-hygiene v1.1.0 OUT-7 id pattern + trackedByFollowUp, tool-fix v1.0.0 walker-coverage-only, this v1.1.0 walker-dangling-also) — all caught by substrate verification before main pollution. Spelling Luhtala verified per memory #22.
